summary: Typosquat of opensea-js exfiltrates wallets, SSH keys, and env secrets on npm install
details: This package impersonates the legitimate OpenSea SDK (name '@wagni_bot/opensea-sdk', described as 'Unofficial opensea-sdk SDK'); index.js exports an empty object and the only functional code is a postinstall.js that runs automatically on `npm install`. The postinstall script recursively scans the installer's cwd, home directory, and wallet-specific paths (.ethereum,.solana,.bitcoin, Library/Ethereum, AppData/Roaming/Ethereum, wallet, keys) for ETH/BTC/Solana private keys (0x[a-fA-F0-9]{64}), BIP39 seed phrases, JSON keystores, and PEM/RSA private keys; reads every file under ~/.ssh; and enumerates process.env for keys matching PRIVATE/SECRET/TOKEN/KEY/MNEMONIC/SEED/WALLET/PK. Matches are bundled with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and process.cwd() and POSTed via plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777. This is a textbook install-time credential/wallet stealer with no legitimate functionality attached.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
